en.extract:
The Madagascar pratincole (Glareola ocularis) is a species of bird in the family Glareolidae.
It is a migratory species, breeding across Madagascar (except in the country’s extreme south-west) before migrating to East Africa during the austral winter (May-August), where it is mainly found near the coast between Somalia and Mozambique. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical seasonally wet or flooded lowland grassland, rivers, freshwater lakes, rocky shores, and intertidal marshes; as it migrates to the coast of East Africa.
It is the rarest species in the Glareola genus, and is threatened by habitat loss.
Its breeding season lasts from October to December, peaking in November.

The '''Madagascar pratincole''' ('''''Glareola ocularis''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Glareolidae]].
It is a migratory species, breeding across [[Madagascar]] (except in the country’s extreme south-west) before migrating to East Africa during the austral winter (May-August), where it is mainly found near the coast between [[Somalia]] and [[Mozambique]]. Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical seasonally wet or flooded lowland [[grassland]], [[river]]s, freshwater [[lake]]s, rocky shores, and intertidal [[marsh]]es; as it migrates to the coast of East Africa.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |date=1987 |title=Breeding Record of the Indian Pratincole Glareola maldivarum in Tochigi Prefecture, Central Honshu |journal=Japanese Journal of Ornithology |volume=35 |issue=4 |pages=166–168 |doi=10.3838/jjo.35.166 |issn=0913-400X|doi-access=free }}</ref>
It is the rarest species in the [[Glareola]] genus,<ref name=":0" /> and is threatened by [[habitat loss]].

Its breeding season lasts from October to December, peaking in November.<ref name=":0" />
